title: fix(gtk): restore middle-click paste when scrollbar is enabled
body:
## Summary

- Fixes middle-click paste (primary selection) not working when GTK scrollbars are enabled
- The `GtkScrolledWindow` was intercepting middle-click events before they reached the terminal Surface

## Solution

- Added a `GestureClick` with `propagation-phase: capture` to the ScrolledWindow that intercepts middle-clicks and forwards them to the Surface
- The gesture is claimed after handling to prevent double-paste when the scrollbar is not visible
- Also disabled `kinetic-scrolling` on the ScrolledWindow as it uses middle-click for autoscroll

## Test plan

- [x] Enable scrollbars (`scrollbar = system` in config)
- [x] Select text in another application to populate primary selection
- [x] Middle-click in Ghostty terminal - should paste once
- [x] Run `seq 1 80` to make scrollbar visible
- [x] Middle-click again - should still paste once

Fixes #10613

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
author: hakonhagland

linked_issues.title: Ubuntu 25.10: Middle-click paste does not work when the GTK scrollbar feature is enabled

issue_comments.body:
For reference: we found the root cause of this issue.                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                              
 The bug only occurs when the GNOME setting `org.gnome.desktop.interface overlay-scrolling` is set to `false` (traditional scrollbars). With the default `true`  (overlay scrollbars), middle-click works correctly.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                              
 When overlay scrolling is disabled, GTK's `ScrolledWindow` handles middle-click events differently, intercepting them before they reach child widgets.        
                                                                                                                                                              
  This fix would still be useful for users who prefer traditional scrollbars, but it's a more limited use case than originally thought.                       
                                                                                                                                                              
  See discussion: https://github.com/ghostty-org/ghostty/discussions/10616
